The liver plays a crucial role in the muscular system by providing essential nutrients and energy to the muscles. It [processes] nutrients from the digestive system and [stores] them for use when needed. The liver also [regulates] the levels of certain chemicals in the blood, which helps maintain the [function] of the muscles. It [produces] enzymes that are used in the breakdown of [fats] and [carbohydrates], which are vital for muscle function. The liver [filters] toxins and waste products from the blood, ensuring that the muscles receive clean and [balanced] nutrients. It [supports] the muscles by supplying oxygen and [nutrients] that are necessary for their [operation]. The liver also [releases] hormones that regulate the [metabolism] of the muscles, ensuring they function properly. Finally, the liver [supports] the muscles by maintaining the [balance] of fluids and [electrolytes] in the body, which is essential for muscle function.

Uncle [supports] the [muscular] system [everyday].
